title: nephelium noronhianum
intro: Nephelium noronhianum, also known as the Noronha rambutan or rambai, is a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the Western Ghats in India and parts of Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Nephelium noronhianum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Nephelium noronhianum include decoctions, infusions, and powdered extracts, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to food products.
